# Directory — Render-Only Multi-Source View

## The mental model

The substrate is authoritative. The rules folder has the rules; the
decisions folder has the decisions; the registry has the canonical
list. But "authoritative" is not the same as "easy to navigate."

A **directory** is a derived view that filters + projects the substrate
into a read-only digest optimized for reading. A "rules directory for
the meta domain" walks `rules/` + the rules `_index.yaml`, selects only
the meta-domain entries, sorts them by stage_label or ID, and writes a
clean digest to `.jos/directories/rules-meta.md`. The directory is the
projected view; the rules themselves are unmoved.

## Why this is its own component-type, not a script output

Three reasons:

1. **It earns governance lifecycle.** Directories that exist informally
   (as Python functions in `knowledge_projection.py`) accumulate
   without review. As components, each directory declares an owner, a
   review cadence, an explicit source-of-truth set, and a filter — so
   the surface stays inspectable instead of drifting into anonymous
   automation.
2. **It is enumerable.** Agents can ask "what directories does this
   venture project?" by reading `directories/_index.yaml`, exactly the
   way they ask "what gates run here?" by reading `instruments/gates/
   _index.yaml`. Without the type, directories are invisible to the
   discovery surface.
3. **It is the natural home for per-domain rule sheets, per-substrate
   cheatsheets, per-checker fleet views.** All three are the same
   pattern. Naming the pattern is what lets future authors add the
   tenth directory without inventing yet another renderer.

## What a Directory IS

- A declared **intent**: "consolidate THIS substrate slice into a digest
  at THIS output path, regenerated on commit."
- A **filter** + **source pointer** set. Sources are paths to other
  substrate (e.g., `rules/_index.yaml`); filters are field selectors
  (e.g., `domain: meta`).
- An **output target** in `.jos/directories/<slug>.md` per consumer
  venture (mirrored from JOS via charter sync).
- A **render template** reference (Jinja, Markdown skeleton, or just
  "default tabular").

## What a Directory is NOT

- **Not authoritative.** The substrate it projects IS the truth;
  hand-edits to the rendered output are reverted on next run.
- **Not an Index.** Indexes are per-substrate-folder, count + enumerate
  one type. Directories cross-cut and can mix types (e.g., a
  "checkers" directory aggregates gates + monitors + sops).
- **Not a Manifest.** Manifests are per-thing, hand-authored, declare
  slots. Directories are auto-rendered, scoped by filter.
- **Not a Registry.** The Registry is THE canonical list (one per
  repo). Directories are many; they slice and project.
- **Not Atlas.** Atlas is the agent-readable global index of EVERYTHING
  visible; directories are scoped + filtered. Atlas may itself be a
  directory under this concept (JOSW2 follow-on consideration).

## Examples

| Directory | Sources | Filter | Output |
|---|---|---|---|
| `JOS-DIR-RULES-META` | `rules/_index.yaml` | `domain: meta` | `.jos/directories/rules-meta.md` |
| `JOS-DIR-RULES-ENG` | `rules/_index.yaml` | `domain: eng` | `.jos/directories/rules-eng.md` |
| `JOS-DIR-RULES-ALL` | `rules/_index.yaml` | (none — by domain) | `.jos/directories/rules-all.md` |
| `JOS-DIR-COMPONENTS` | every substrate `_index.yaml` | (none) | `.jos/COMPONENTS-CHEATSHEET.md` |
| `JOS-DIR-CHECKERS` | `check-results/_registry.yaml` | (none) | `.jos/directories/checkers.md` |

## The quartet (per JOS-SPEC-021 + this concept)

| | Authoritative? | Cardinality | Hand-edited? |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Registry** | YES — the canonical list | ONE per repo (`.jos/registry.yaml`) | YES (or sync-rendered if so configured) |
| **Index** | YES — per-folder counts + pointers | MANY (one per substrate folder) | Edit OR auto-generated; either way authoritative for that folder |
| **Manifest** | YES — per-thing declared truth | MANY (one per declared thing) | YES |
| **Directory** | NO — projected view | MANY | NEVER — render-only |

Per JOS-SPEC-021 strict no-mix rules: never confuse one for another.
This concept locks the fourth row.

## Relationship to JOS-C14 + JOS-P07 (Ambient Discoverability)

Discoverability requires the shelf (Registry) AND the labeled aisles
(Directories). The Registry holds the inventory; the Directories are
the signs that say "rules for meta-domain, this way." Without them,
the shelf has 700 entries in arrival order and nobody reads it.

## How directories regenerate

Each directory artifact's frontmatter has `auto_regen: true | false`.
- `true`: a post-commit hook (or charter-sync run) walks
  `directories/_index.yaml` and re-renders each. Output overwritten.
- `false`: directory regenerates only on explicit
  `scripts/jos render-directory <id>`. Used when render is expensive
  or external.

Per JOS-SPEC-023 §regeneration, the rendered output carries the
canonical header:

```
<!-- Auto-rendered by JOS Directory render. Source: <directory-id>.
     Do NOT edit. Re-run via `scripts/jos render-directories`. -->
```
